questions.question: Define inheritance in Java and explain its significance.
keyPoints: Inheritance is a mechanism where a new class acquires properties of an existing class
Uses the 'extends' keyword to implement the IS-A relationship
Promotes code reuse, extensibility, and polymorphism

questions.question: Explain the concept of inheritance in Java with an example. Discuss the benefits and key features of inheritance.
keyPoints: Definition of inheritance and its significance
Example demonstrating the use of the 'extends' keyword
Explanation of the IS-A relationship and single class inheritance
Discussion on the benefits of inheritance (code reuse, extensibility, polymorphism)
Key features of inheritance (member access rules, private members not accessible)

questions.question: Compare and contrast the use of 'super' keyword and 'this' keyword in Java inheritance.
keyPoints: Explanation of the 'super' keyword and its usage
Explanation of the 'this' keyword and its usage
Comparison of the two keywords in the context of inheritance
Discussion on when to use each keyword
Example demonstrating the use of both keywords

questions.question: List the key features of inheritance in Java.
keyPoints: IS-A relationship
Single class inheritance
Member access rules
Private members not accessible
Code reuse, extensibility, and polymorphism

questions.question: Discuss the importance of method overriding and dynamic method dispatch in Java inheritance.
keyPoints: Explanation of method overriding and its significance
Explanation of dynamic method dispatch and its significance
Discussion on the benefits of using method overriding and dynamic method dispatch
Example demonstrating the use of both concepts
Key considerations when using method overriding and dynamic method dispatch
